topic: Part 8b — Dome Author's Fair-Auditor List (pre-integration review of expansion-draft)
our_argument_summary: EXP-184 adjudicates the dome author's five-item 'What A Fair Auditor Should Actually Test' list from review-response.html (2026-04-17). Verdicts: (1) ADOPTED PARTIAL — claim_key annotations yes, 'collisions resolved' no; (2) ADOPTED FULL as steel-man — sequenced-read table at WIN-069, independence critique unchanged; (3) ADOPTED FULL — four-layer eclipse stack, explicit Tier 3 threshold; (4) ADOPTED FULL — resolution criteria for WIN-058b, dated history preserved; (5) REJECTED with stronger symmetric replacement standard — dated citations + always-current engagement + preserved change log. Plus a structural-meta section (8b.6) noting the list shipped in the same 24-hour window as the API-level concessions. 2326 words (over the 1200-1800 target).
note_on_integration_state: EXP-184 has ALREADY been integrated into data/sections.json as part8b (verified: html matches replacement_html byte-for-byte, and build-scripts/generate-html.js already renders part8b at line 1655 with TOC entry at line 1368). So this 'pre-integration' review is being written against live content. All findings below apply to the integrated section as deployed.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No new calculations; adjudicative prose only. Cites (a) existing wins.json verdicts for WIN-058/058b/062/068/069/011, (b) predictions.json entries for PRED-095/PRED-TIER-2, (c) poller change archive chg-20260417-1620-* for the sequencing observation, (d) cross-references to Section 8.17, Timestamp Error tab, Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit), Kill-Shot #1, Section 4.1.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: n/a — prose section, not a win or prediction
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 8b.6 explicitly says 'we note the sequencing without attributing intent' — this is a hedging device that pre-empts the most obvious bias-accusation, though it also creates room for the dome author to say we're insinuating without committing.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: Cross-reference labels in part8b are factually wrong in at least two places (see critical/major holes below): (a) 'Kill-Shot #1' is used 3x to label what is actually Section 4.1 (the 47.69σ independence audit); the real Kill-Shot #1 in Part 5 is the Sydney-Perth distance test. (b) 'pending integration via EXP-183' appears 2x referencing Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit), but Part 4.2 is already live in part4.html (EXP-183 was integrated). (c) Part 4 now has TWO subsections numbered '4.2' (the original 'August 2026 Eclipse' and the new 'Registry Framing Audit'), which is the exact same display-level collision pattern 8b.1 criticizes the dome author for. (d) L3 eclipse-layer citation muddles PRED-TIER-2 (recycled gravity) with the −17 to −21 nT magnetic threshold that belongs to PRED-TIER-1/PRED-095.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Prose section; not monitored by monitor.py. No API/URL fetch applies.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Section is a point-by-point response to the dome author's fair-auditor list; it does not relabel standard physics.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Our response to a dome page published 2026-04-17 is necessarily written after the page exists; that is not 'post-hoc' in the technical sense used for scored WINs.

holes_found.description: 'Kill-Shot #1' misattribution. Part 8b uses the label 'Kill-Shot #1' three times (in 8b.2 TLDR, 8b.2 (d) closer, and 8b.7 closing cross-reference) to refer to 'the independence audit of the scored-domains dashboard' / 'Fisher σ-combination requires each claim to have an independent data generator.' That analysis lives in Section 4.1 (The 28-Domain / 47.69σ Dashboard). The ACTUAL Kill-Shot #1 in our Part 5 table is the Sydney–Perth distance test. Verified: Part 5 Kill-Shot Verdict Summary has Kill-Shots 1–7, with Kill-Shot 1 = Sydney–Perth (verdict Misleading), not the independence audit. No other section in the review labels the 47.69σ analysis as 'Kill-Shot #1'. The embedded anchor Section 4.1 in 8b.2 does point to the right Part 4.1 subsection, so the LINK resolves correctly; only the TEXT LABEL 'Kill-Shot #1' is wrong. A dome defender pointing this out scores a cheap 'they can't keep their own kill-shot numbering straight' rhetorical hit that undermines the whole section.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace all 3 occurrences of 'Kill-Shot #1' in part8b with 'Section 4.1' or 'the Part 4.1 independence audit' (consistent with the anchor text already used in 8b.2). Alternatively, IF the intent is to elevate the 47.69σ independence critique to a formal Kill-Shot entry in Part 5, file that as a separate EXP and ship the renumbering before part8b references it as a Kill-Shot. Do not ship the current text unchanged.

holes_found.description: L3 eclipse-layer PRED-ID muddle in 8b.3. The layered stack says: 'L3 (PRED-095, PRED-TIER-2): PRED-095 is genuinely prospective... PRED-TIER-2 is recycled (restates WIN-011 with 1997 data). Under adoption we publish an explicit Tier 3 pass/fail threshold — Pass = observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations...' This conflates two independent prediction strands. Verified against data/predictions.json: PRED-095 is the magnetic-field prediction (−17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage), PRED-TIER-1 is the dome's umbra-locked magnetic ΔB prediction (also −17 to −21 nT, ≥3 sites), PRED-TIER-2 is the recycled gravity claim (10–13 µGal at C1/C4), and PRED-TIER-3 is the Level-3 SG gravity test (also 10–13 µGal gravity). The dome author's point-3 'Tier 3 falsifier' most charitably refers to PRED-TIER-3 in his own tier system, and the −17 to −21 nT threshold applies ONLY to PRED-TIER-1 / PRED-095 (both magnetic). EXP-184 puts PRED-TIER-2 in the L3 slot and then quotes a magnetic-field threshold — these belong to different predictions. A dome defender can say 'you don't understand my tier system.'
holes_found.recommendation: Separate magnetic from gravity. Suggested rewrite: 'L3 (magnetic-ΔB falsifier — PRED-TIER-1 / PRED-095): Pass = −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments. L3b (gravity falsifier — PRED-TIER-2 [recycled] / PRED-TIER-3 [pending]): Pass = 10–13 µGal at C1/C4 contact phases in SG Level-3 residuals.' Then keep the existing critique that a recycled prediction is not a prospective falsifier.

holes_found.description: 'Pending integration via EXP-183' is already stale — Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit) is live in data/sections.json as of the EXP-183 integration (verified: 4.2 Registry Framing Audit h2 present in part4.html). Yet part8b still says 'Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit, pending integration via EXP-183)' twice (in 8b.6 and 8b.7). The notes_for_decider explicitly warned to soften or remove these once Part 4.2 lands ('Current part8b language already says pending integration via EXP-183 in three places — update once Part 4.2 lands'); that update was not applied at integration time. Readers who click through will find a live section while our prose tells them it's pending. This is exactly the 'cite current wording' failure mode point 5 rejects — and it is now in force in the section that does the rejecting.
holes_found.recommendation: Drop 'pending integration via EXP-183' from both remaining occurrences. Replace with plain 'Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit)' or 'the Registry Framing Audit in Section 4.2'. Also delete the now-stale clause in 8b.7 integration-schedule prose that implies EXP-183 is still pending.

holes_found.description: Part 4 now contains TWO subsections numbered '4.2' — the original '4.2 The August 2026 Eclipse: A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction' (with 4.2.1–4.2.6 subheadings) AND the newer '4.2 Registry Framing Audit — API Concessions vs Visible Headline' (from EXP-183 integration). This is a display-level collision, the exact failure mode 8b.1 criticizes the dome author for ('so an auditor does not merge win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule into a single contradiction'). Every EXP-184 reference to 'Part 4.2' is now ambiguous — a reader does not know whether we mean Eclipse or Registry-Framing. While the root cause is an EXP-183 numbering choice, part8b amplifies the damage by citing 'Part 4.2' without disambiguation. The dome author has a ready-made rhetorical rejoinder: 'you critique my duplicate WIN labels while publishing duplicate section labels.'
holes_found.recommendation: Two parallel fixes. (a) File a separate issue to resolve the Part 4 numbering collision — likely by renumbering the Registry Framing Audit to 4.6 (next available) and updating all cross-refs. (b) In the meantime, patch part8b's Part 4.2 references to use the disambiguating title, e.g., 'Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit)' not bare 'Part 4.2'. The second fix is EXP-184's scope; the first is a project-level patch.

holes_found.description: 8b.4 TLDR misrepresents our verdicts. TLDR says 'Our WIN-058 and WIN-058b already reflect pending / not-demonstrated.' But data/wins.json shows WIN-058 verdict = 'Misleading' (not 'pending') and WIN-058b verdict = 'Not Demonstrated'. The body paragraph correctly states WIN-058 as 'Misleading', so the TLDR contradicts the body. Per the TLDR review rule, TLDR factual errors are major-severity; this one is a mid-moderate because a charitable reader parses 'pending' as the dome author's status (not ours), but non-charitable readers and the dome author will cite it.
holes_found.recommendation: Change TLDR to: 'Our WIN-058 and WIN-058b already reflect Misleading / Not Demonstrated; we add explicit resolution criteria.' This matches the body and avoids mixing our verdicts with the dome author's 'pending' status.

holes_found.description: 8b.5 publishes three commitments (dated citations / always-current engagement / preserved change log) and says 'This is already in force across this review; this section makes it explicit.' That is a falsifiable claim the dome author can probe instance-by-instance. An informal scan of the current review's citations shows many do NOT carry an explicit 'as of [date]' label — e.g., citations to peer-reviewed papers, the NASA/NOAA references in Part 7, general DOI links. If the dome author finds a single citation without an 'as of' label, he gets to say 'your own rule, already violated.' Either the commitment needs softening, or we need a project-level citation audit before this section ships as a promise.
holes_found.recommendation: Option A (safest): soften to 'We commit to adopting this standard going forward; an audit of existing citations to bring them into compliance is tracked as a separate issue.' Option B: file an issue for a project-wide citation-labelling audit and run it before part8b ships. Option C: leave the text and accept the exposure — but flag that the dome author will probably name a violating citation within 48 hours of publication, and we need a pre-written response.

holes_found.description: Terminology slip in 8b.6: '…landed in the same multi-file push window…' Three commits are named (6e06efb, 5021eec, a57e731) but the text treats them as a single push. A git 'push' is one operation; three separate commits can be in one push OR in three separate pushes. EXP-184 does not verify which — the poller merely detected them all at once at 2026-04-17T16:21:52Z. 8b.6 elsewhere uses 'same 24-hour window' which is the defensible framing. Mixing 'push window' and '24-hour window' in the same paragraph invites a narrow rebuttal: 'they weren't one push, they were three.'
holes_found.recommendation: Replace 'multi-file push window' with 'same-detection-window' or '24-hour window' — whichever is supported by the change evidence. Pick one and use it consistently in 8b.6.

holes_found.description: The '0.4%' Nullarbor figure in 8b.2 body is not directly sourced in the current WIN-069 text. WIN-069 detail_evidence says 'road-to-geodesic ratio approaches 1.00 on its dead-straight central segment (~146 km), though the full Norseman–Ceduna crossing computes closer to ~1.07'. '0.4%' is a reasonable numerical translation of 'approaches 1.00' but not the exact phrasing in our own WIN-069. A dome defender could ask 'cite your 0.4% figure.'
holes_found.recommendation: Either (a) soften to match WIN-069: 'the Nullarbor Plain straight-road segment has near-zero deviation from the globe geodesic on its ~146 km central section' — OR (b) source the 0.4% figure to an underlying calculation or dataset and cite it. Not a blocker but worth tightening.

holes_found.description: Word-count overshoot. EXP-184 hits 2326 text words vs. the 1200–1800 human-note target — 29% over the upper bound. notes_on_length acknowledges this and names 8b.0 preamble and 8b.6 meta as trim targets. The section is coherent but loose in places (multiple paragraphs could be tightened without losing substance). Not worth blocking integration over, but a follow-up trim pass would strengthen it.
holes_found.recommendation: Optional follow-up EXP: 15% trim focused on 8b.0 preamble and 8b.6 meta, preserving (c)-under-adoption paragraphs which were the designated hero.

holes_found.description: Build-pipeline-note line numbers drift. EXP-184's build_pipeline_note targets 'line 1651-1652 ... renderSectionFromJson calls' and 'line 1367-1369 ... TOC entry'. Actual current locations are line 1655 (render call) and line 1368 (TOC). Off by 3–4 lines. Not a blocker because the integration actually happened correctly, but future analysts generating build-pipeline notes should either (a) state line ranges less precisely ('between part8 and part9') or (b) re-check against the current file at integration time.

The '(c) is the hero' note in 8b.0 is legible to internal readers but may puzzle external readers. 'Hero' is an internal content-editing term; the public-facing TLDR does not need to advertise editorial structure. Consider trimming the meta-language from 8b.0 or moving it to a notes_for_decider field.
holes_found.recommendation: Remove the '(c) is the hero' sentence from the 8b.0 body or reword to 'the concrete-changes clause (c) carries the substantive adjudication'.

stronger_arguments.description: On point-5 rejection, the structural case is stronger than the rhetorical case. Scientific nomenclature bodies (IUPAC, zoological / botanical codes, ICD coding, CVE catalogs) universally preserve superseded / deprecated entries with dated metadata precisely because the trajectory of names and definitions IS evidence. The dome author's 'only current wording' rule runs directly counter to the entire scientific practice of versioned nomenclature. Make this the main argument — the 'memory-hole regime' framing is good rhetoric but easy to dismiss as hyperbole; the IUPAC/CVE parallel is harder to dismiss.
sources: IUPAC recommendations on element-name changes preserving historical designations
CVE MITRE policy on retaining deprecated CVE IDs
Code of Zoological Nomenclature Art. 23 (preserving superseded names)

stronger_arguments.description: On the claim_key partial adoption (8b.1), strengthen by noting web-accessibility grounds. WCAG 2.1 AA §2.4.6 (Headings and Labels) and §3.2.4 (Consistent Identification) effectively require that identical labels identify identical content. The dome's wins.html has TWO visible WIN-058 blocks with different content — this fails WCAG compliance independently of our review's adjudicative stance. That gives us a second, neutral authority to cite.

On 8b.6 sequencing, the stronger version of the argument is narrower and more factual: regardless of intent, the rule 'judge by current wording' and the current wording itself ship together → anyone applying the rule cannot evaluate whether the rule was designed around the text or the text around the rule. That's a methodological point about the audit-rule's self-testability, not about the author's motives. The section already nearly makes this argument; sharpening it removes the easy 'you're insinuating' counter.

kernel_of_truth.description: Three of the dome author's five criteria are genuinely good-faith audit asks and meaningfully improve precision. claim_key disambiguation is the right engineering fix for the label collision problem. Layered eclipse audits force explicit falsifier thresholds, which the dome site previously avoided. Explicit resolution criteria for pending items is orthodox scientific practice. Even the rejected point 5 identifies a real failure mode — attacking deleted text IS a strawman pattern. EXP-184 credits all of this correctly.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: EXP-184 does NOT over-credit — the verdicts are measured (3 full adoptions, 1 partial, 1 rejection). The critical-severity hole (Kill-Shot #1 misattribution) does not touch the adjudicative structure itself; it's a labelling bug in the cross-references. Once the holes are patched, the core adjudication holds up.

recommended_action_rationale: Core structure sound; adjudications accurate per current wins.json / predictions.json; counterpoints adequately considered. But the 3x 'Kill-Shot #1' mislabel is CRITICAL and must be fixed in place, and the 2x 'pending integration via EXP-183' is MAJOR and stale. These are find-and-replace fixes, not rewrites. The L3 PRED-muddle is also a localized fix. With those plus the 8b.4 TLDR correction, the section ships cleanly.
deeper_analysis:
Three layers of analysis:

(1) ADJUDICATION LAYER — the five verdicts (3 full adoptions, 1 partial, 1 rejection) are individually defensible against the current dome text and our own data. The partial-adoption of point 1 correctly preserves the display-collision critique while accepting the machine-readable fix. The steel-man adoption of point 2 correctly retains the independence critique regardless of narrative sequencing. The full adoption of point 3 correctly restructures our eclipse content into layered presentation while preserving the anchor-is-a-fit critique. The full adoption of point 4 correctly codifies resolution criteria while preserving the dated history. The rejection of point 5 correctly identifies the absolutist reading and proposes a stricter symmetric rule.

(2) CROSS-REFERENCE LAYER — this is where the section weakens. 'Kill-Shot #1' is mislabeled as the 47.69σ independence audit when the actual Kill-Shot #1 is Sydney–Perth. 'Pending integration via EXP-183' is already stale. 'Part 4.2' is ambiguous because Part 4 has two subsections numbered 4.2. The L3 eclipse layer conflates PRED-TIER-2 with the -17 to -21 nT threshold that belongs elsewhere. These are all labelling / cross-reference bugs, not substantive errors — but they accumulate.

(3) EXPOSURE LAYER — 8b.5's 'already in force across this review' commitment is the single biggest publication risk. It creates a unilateral falsifiable claim the dome author can probe instance-by-instance. If he finds one citation without an 'as of [date]' label, he gets a free rhetorical win. Either soften or pre-audit.

Overall assessment: strong adjudication with patchable cross-reference and exposure issues. Minor_edit recommended, not major_rewrite.

citations_failed: 'Kill-Shot #1 (the independence audit of the scored-domains dashboard)' — Kill-Shot #1 in Part 5 table is Sydney–Perth distance, NOT the independence audit. Label is WRONG in 3 places (8b.2 TLDR, 8b.2 (d), 8b.7).

'Nullarbor Plain straight-road segment deviates only 0.4% from the globe geodesic' — WIN-069 text says 'approaches 1.00 on its dead-straight central segment (~146 km)', not 0.4%. The 0.4% figure is a plausible translation but not sourced in the cited WIN.
'Part 4.2 (Registry Framing Audit, pending integration via EXP-183)' — Part 4.2 is LIVE; label 'pending' is stale. 2 occurrences.
'L3 (PRED-095, PRED-TIER-2)' with a -17 to -21 nT threshold — threshold is magnetic (PRED-095 / PRED-TIER-1), but PRED-TIER-2 is recycled gravity. The L3 layer conflates two prediction tracks.
build_pipeline_note line numbers (1651-1652 for render, 1367-1369 for TOC) vs actual (1655, 1368) — minor drift.
citations_unchecked: Live review-response.html on john09289.github.io was not re-fetched in this review; the verbatim text was verified against EXP-184's dome_source_verified_content snapshot (2026-04-18T13:30Z). If the page has been edited since, part8b's quotes may be stale.

Dome-site URLs (wins.html#win-069, coordinates.html#australia-v13-scaffold, model.html#southern-validation-australia, killshot.html#killshot-southern-metric-expansion) — anchor targets not independently re-fetched.
Chapman 1933 paper specifics (cited indirectly via WIN-068 finding field) — not independently verified here beyond wins.json content.

advocate_mode_strongest_defense: If I were the dome author responding to part8b, I would write: 'Three of five points adopted — thank you. But note (a) your section cites *Kill-Shot #1* three times for an analysis that is actually Section 4.1; your own Part 5 shows Kill-Shot #1 is the Sydney–Perth test. (b) Your section says my Part 4.2 Registry Framing Audit is *pending integration* while linking to the live section. (c) Your Part 4 itself has duplicate section numbers (two 4.2 subsections) — the exact collision pattern you criticize in my WIN labels. (d) Your L3 eclipse layer attaches a magnetic threshold to a gravity prediction. Before I adopt your rejection of my point 5, apply your stated discipline to your own section 8b.' Every one of those charges lands on a specific hole this review has flagged. Patching before the dome author reads the section is worth doing.
cross_win_consistency_notes: Part 8b coordinates with Section 8.17 (catalogs review-response.html as part of AI-perception-management layer), Part 4.2 Registry Framing Audit (the general API-vs-human-text pattern), Timestamp Error tab (OTS scoping), Kill-Shot numbering in Part 5, and individual WIN rows (058, 058b, 062, 068, 069, 011). The Section 8.17 and Timestamp Error cross-references are solid. The Part 4.2 cross-reference is ambiguous (two sections share the number) and labeled stale ('pending integration'). The Kill-Shot cross-reference is outright wrong. The WIN-058 TLDR mixes dome-side 'pending' with our 'Misleading'. Coordination work is needed.
